title: Design Parking Lot
description: Design an object-oriented parking lot system. Must handle different vehicle types (car, truck, van, motorcycle), multiple floors, entry/exit gates, and billing.
hints: Core entities: ParkingLot, ParkingFloor, ParkingSpot (Small, Medium, Large), Vehicle, Ticket, Payment.
Use Inheritance for Vehicle types and ParkingSpot types.
Consider the Strategy pattern for the billing system (hourly, flat, weekend rates).
Finding the nearest available spot: use a min-heap per floor, keyed by spot number.
Thread Safety: use synchronized blocks or ReentrantLock on spot allocation to handle concurrent entries.

title: Design BookMyShow / Movie Ticket Booking System
description: Design an online movie ticket booking system that handles theatre selection, seat reservation, and payments.
hints: Entities: Movie, Theatre, Screen, Show, Seat, Booking, Payment, User.
Seat locking: Use a temporary hold with TTL (e.g., 10 minutes) to prevent double-booking.
Concurrency: Optimistic locking on seat status. First successful CAS (Compare-And-Swap) wins.
Consider Observer pattern to notify users about seat availability changes.

title: Design ATM Machine
description: Design an ATM system that supports withdrawals, deposits, balance checks, and fund transfers. Handle multiple denominations and insufficient funds.
hints: Entities: ATM, Account, Card, Transaction, CashDispenser, Bank.
Use State pattern for ATM states: Idle, CardInserted, PinEntered, AmountEntered, CashDispensed.
CashDispenser: Use Chain of Responsibility to dispense different denominations (2000, 500, 200, 100).
Validate: card, PIN, account balance before dispensing.

title: Design Elevator System
description: Design an elevator system for a multi-floor building with multiple elevators. Handle concurrent requests efficiently.
hints: Entities: Building, Elevator, Floor, Request (internal/external), ElevatorController.
Use Strategy pattern for scheduling algorithms: FCFS, SCAN, LOOK.
State pattern for elevator: Moving Up, Moving Down, Idle, Door Open.
Controller assigns the nearest elevator or the one already moving in the request direction.

title: Design Snake and Ladder Game
description: Design the classic Snake and Ladder board game. Support multiple players, custom board configurations, and dice rolling.
hints: Entities: Board, Cell, Snake, Ladder, Player, Dice, Game.
Board is a 1D/2D grid of 100 cells. Snakes move you down, Ladders move you up.
Use a Queue of players to manage turns.
Game loop: roll dice -> move player -> check for snake/ladder -> check win condition.

title: Design Hotel Management System
description: Design a hotel management system supporting room booking, check-in/out, room service, and billing.
hints: Entities: Hotel, Floor, Room (Single, Double, Suite), Guest, Booking, RoomService, Invoice.
Room states: Available, Booked, Occupied, Under Maintenance.
Use Factory pattern for creating different room types.
Billing: Composite pattern to aggregate room charges + services.

title: Design Library Management System
description: Design a system to manage library operations including book cataloging, member management, borrowing, and fine calculation.
hints: Entities: Library, Book, BookItem (physical copy), Member, Librarian, Lending, Fine.
A Book can have multiple BookItems (copies). Each BookItem tracks its status.
BookItem states: Available, Lent, Reserved, Lost.
Use Observer pattern: notify members when a reserved book becomes available.

title: Design LRU Cache
description: Implement an LRU (Least Recently Used) cache with O(1) get and put operations. Handle eviction when capacity is full.
hints: Use a HashMap for O(1) key-to-node lookup + Doubly Linked List for O(1) reordering.
On get: move the accessed node to the head (most recently used).
On put: if key exists, update and move to head. If new, add to head. If capacity exceeded, remove tail.
The tail of the linked list is the LRU element.

title: Design Tic-Tac-Toe
description: Design a Tic-Tac-Toe game. Support n x n board, two players, and determine the winner in O(1) per move.
hints: Entities: Board, Player, Game.
O(1) win check: maintain row count, col count, diagonal count, anti-diagonal count per player.
On each move, increment the player's count for that row, col, and diagonal (if applicable).
If any count reaches n, that player wins.

title: Design Rate Limiter
description: Design a rate limiter that restricts the number of requests a client can make within a time window.
hints: Common algorithms: Token Bucket, Sliding Window, Fixed Window, Leaky Bucket.
Token Bucket: tokens are added at a fixed rate. Each request consumes a token. If no tokens, reject.
Sliding Window Log: store timestamps. Count requests in the last N seconds.
For distributed systems, use Redis with atomic operations.

title: Design E-Commerce System (Amazon/Walmart)
description: Design an e-commerce platform. Handle product catalog, user accounts, shopping cart, checkout, order management, and inventory.
hints: Entities: User, Product, Category, ShoppingCart, CartItem, Order, OrderItem, Payment, Shipment, Address.
Order states: Placed, Confirmed, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled, Returned.
Concurrency: Use optimistic locking on inventory. Decrement stock atomically on order placement.
Use Strategy pattern for different payment methods (Credit Card, UPI, Wallet).

title: Design File System
description: Design an in-memory file system. Support creating, deleting, and searching files and directories.
hints: Use Composite pattern: File and Directory are both FileSystemEntry.
Directory contains a list of FileSystemEntry (children).
Path resolution: split path by '/' and traverse the tree.
Support ls, mkdir, addContentToFile, readContentFromFile.
